cot_prefix: Okay, let's read through the text to answer this question about the mother. The question is asking what we know about the mother according to the text, with the choices being A to D. First, the story starts by saying the mother wanted a flower garden but was too busy raising her five children: Matthew, Marvin, the twins Alisa and Alan, and Helen. She mentions that money and time were tight. She often told her children, "Do you see a money tree outside? Money doesn't grow on trees, you know," when they wanted something expensive. Then, after her children grew up and left home, on Mother's Day, she found a tree in her yard with $100 in dollar bills taped to it. Marvin wrote a note saying "IOU eight hours of digging time," meaning he would help dig a garden bed. The other children also gave her gardening tools and items. The garden turned out well, and she feels her children are with her when she tends to the flowers. The last sentence says she's still not sure money grows on trees, but she knows love does. Now, looking at the options: (A) She is a bad-tempered woman. The text doesn't mention her being bad-tempered. In fact, she's described as a dedicated mother who worked hard for her children. So A is likely incorrect. (B) She believes money can grow on a tree now. The story ends with her saying she's not sure money grows on trees, but she knows love does. So B is incorrect. (C) She is a writer. There's no mention of her being a writer or writing in the text. So C is not supported. (D) She is hard-working in housekeeping. The text describes her as being too busy raising children to have a garden, and she had to say no to expensive items because of money constraints. This suggests she was hard-working in managing her household
